title: Manage external dependencies with bzlmod
description: Declare external modules in MODULE.bazel with explicit versions so the dependency set is reproducible and resolvable.
body: Use when adding or upgrading an external dependency in a modern Bazel project. Do not mix ad hoc WORKSPACE rules with bzlmod for the same dependency. Actions: add the module and version to MODULE.bazel; let resolution pick compatible versions; commit the lock so the graph is reproducible. Validation signals include a clean checkout resolving the same versions and a build that does not depend on host-installed packages. Risk boundary: unpinned or duplicated dependency declarations cause version skew and supply-chain ambiguity, so keep one source of truth and review version bumps.
